title: Java Records
description: Declare a compact immutable data carrier with generated accessors and object contracts.
learningGoals: Declare a record and use its generated component accessors.
Normalize constructor arguments with a compact constructor.
Recall that records generate equals, hashCode, and toString from their components.
Implement an interface from a record declaration.

choices: An exception is thrown at runtime
completionChecks.explanation: The compact constructor assigns the stripped, lower-case value to the email component before the generated accessor returns it.

completionChecks.prompt: Which Badge declaration fails to compile?
choices: record Badge(String email, int level) implements Comparable<Badge> { public int compareTo(Badge other) { return 0; } }
record Badge(String email, int level) { void email() { } }
record Badge(String email, int level) { public Badge { email = email.strip(); } }
completionChecks.explanation: A record component already declares a public accessor with the component name, so a void email() method conflicts with the generated String email() accessor.

completionChecks.prompt: What happens when new Badge(" user@example.com ", 0) runs?
choices: A Badge is created with level 1
Compilation fails because compact constructors cannot validate
completionChecks.explanation: The compact constructor normalizes the email but then rejects any level below 1 by throwing IllegalArgumentException.
